MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Pamela Stevens of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Husqvarna Professional Outdoor Products, Inc.
Steven M. Harowitz of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas Luka, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HUSQVARNA PROFESSIONAL OUTDOOR PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Husqvarna Professional Outdoor Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Davis create a triable issue whether Mr. Luka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Davis' declaration are overruled. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant's separate statement contains no facts that discuss anything regarding punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR # 8875 (503/TLJ)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne M. Payne, Esq., of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-438-5970) specially appearing for defendant / moving party Power Equipment Company
Steven M. Harowitz, Esq., of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) for plaintiff / opposing party Richard Theodore Luka

RULING:
DEFENDANT POWER EQUIPMENT COMPANY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing defendant Power Equipment Company's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiffs failed to satisfy their burden of demonstrating that Power Equipment Company had the requisite contacts with California in order to subject it to personal jurisdiction. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance to conduct jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiffs made no showing that their proposed discovery might (much less, would) produce evidence of any contacts of Power Equipment Company with California. Moreover, the case has been pending for almost 28 months and there has been no explanation why plaintiffs are, at what should be near the conclusion of their case, requesting jurisdictional discovery based solely on a hope that some information may be uncovered to enable them to proceed against one of the defendants, which inevitably further delays the case as to all other remaining defendants. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
